notes.note: The Senate was voting honours and supports tied to Plancus's standing and his designated consulship; Cicero explains he could not attend the relevant session in safety.

notes.anchor: in such utter impunity of swords
notes.note: Antony, consul in 44 BC, had packed Rome with armed bodyguards after the Ides of March; Cicero portrays the Senate as cowed by soldiers who hear and intimidate the speakers at close range.
